# social-culture

The file explores how intermediary labor in cultural production—from literary typists to identity verification systems—becomes structurally invisible, with contributions erased or absorbed into final products. It examines asymmetric legibility (users maximally visible, data processing opaque) as the core mechanism enabling both institutional surveillance and the collapse of Constraint/Gift boundaries. The recurring insight is that friction signals cultural value, and transparency about provenance—not isolation—is the genuine solution to detecting and valuing authentic human effort.
